source.title: Clubhouse Map Setup Guide (Siege X) - How To Setup and Defend ALL Sites
source.notes: Recorded on Siege X (post-rework Clubhouse). Generic ranked setups from creator 'Littleton', not pro-league lineups. Auto-generated captions have several garbled operator names — flagged inline where the transcript audio was ambiguous. Room/callout names are the creator's informal callouts.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce these regardless of whether you barricade the tunnel door. When repelling/holding early, check the angle where your feet could be shot from the drone so you can stand safely.

reinforcements.location: Rotate wall between Armory side and Church (double wall)
reinforcements.note: Some setups (especially pro-influenced ranked lobbies) reinforce this wall so attackers can't open the hatch and get an angle on the Church double wall. Creator disagrees with reinforcing it — prefers to keep it soft so defenders can hold the hatch drop angle from here instead.

reinforcements.location: Church, two reinforcements (left and right of the church double-wall, middle left soft)
reinforcements.note: Reinforce left and right, leave the middle soft (especially if a mirror is played there) so you can head-hole the middle to fight onto Main Stairs.
Keeping this hatch closed is the single biggest factor in winning basement — described as the easiest of the four sites if this stays shut.
Reinforce the middle wall in Blue (creator's non-standard preference is to also put the rotate in the middle rather than bottom-left — see rotates).

headHoles.location: Church wall (left/right reinforced, middle left soft)
headHoles.note: Leave head holes here to fight onto Main Stairs — without this, attackers pushing down Main Stairs only have to worry about two angles before walking out freely; this adds a third angle and more peek opportunities for defense. Top of this wall is soft and can be shot over toward bottom of Main Stairs.
Kept deliberately short (not a long sightline) so it only reveals attacker ankles running toward Moto without giving attackers a reciprocal prone sightline down the corridor from bottom Main Stairs.

headHoles.location: Back Armory, box line of sight
headHoles.note: Standing on the box here lets you see Four and bottom Main Stairs — only relevant while the kitchen hatch stays closed. Also usable at round start to catch a Banshee/Fenrir repelling upside down onto the hatch for a free kill before hatches open.

rotates.note: Connects Armory side to Church, holds the hatch drop angle, and lets you rotate to Blue from back here if the kitchen hatch is closed.

rotates.location: Small mini rotate, right-hand side near Armory
rotates.note: Lets you fight onto Main Stairs from here and gives a quick out if attackers start fusing/hard-breaching Kitchen, or a fast dart to rotate up into Kitchen once that hatch opens.

rotates.location: Blue rotate, MIDDLE of the wall (non-standard)
rotates.note: Creator's contentious pick: most setups reinforce the middle/right wall and put the rotate bottom-left, but he puts the rotate in the middle instead. Reasoning: if attackers pop a mirror on the standard wall, they can shoot the top of that wall from the platform and see all the way back to the mirror; a middle rotate can't be seen from the hatch at all (the air conditioning unit blocks it), whereas the bottom-left rotate can be seen from the hatch. Trade-off: the middle rotate gives attackers a slightly wider view into Church.

rotates.location: Blue → Church, imaginary boundary at the air conditioning unit
rotates.note: If the Blue hatch is opened, do not go past an imaginary line around the AC unit or you'll be visible from the hatch.

hatches.note: One of the three basement hatches, discussed together with Moto/Church hatch and Blue hatch.
Keeping this closed makes the whole basement site significantly easier to defend; once open, holding becomes much harder even though the surrounding area is a strong stronghold.
Cannot be seen from the middle rotate position (creator's preferred setup) due to the AC unit blocking the sightline; can be seen from the standard bottom-left rotate position instead.

gadgetPlacements.location: Either of the two Dirt Tunnel walls
gadgetPlacements.note: Wait until you hear attackers placing hard breach charges (thermite/Ace-style) on the walls, then throw an electroclaw for a quick trick during roughly the first 30 seconds. Afterward, relocate the claw to cover the Kitchen hatch and Moto hatch instead — you won't hold Dirt forever, but stealing a hard breach charge's value is worth it.

gadgetPlacements.location: Dirt Tunnel, hidden behind wooden barriers/corners
gadgetPlacements.note: Hide proxy-style gadgets behind corners using the wooden barriers for concealment, so attackers coming down don't spot them — gives early warning someone's there.

Source audio said 'a banshee, a fen rear' — this maps to Melusi's Banshee sonic device and/or Fenrir's dread mine, both viable info/trap tools here. Goo mines (Lesion) are called out as not reliable enough for this specific job; prox mines are called 'probably okay' but some kind of info gadget is considered mandatory in Dirt regardless of whether the tunnel is barricaded.
Stand on the box using the red numbers as a placement guide to hit a cage/gadget trick — usable at start of round.

gadgetPlacements.location: Blue, on the step near the platform and up on the oil pit steps
gadgetPlacements.note: Source audio said 'legion' with 'goo mines' — resolved to Lesion (Defense-side, Gu mines are her signature gadget; 'Legion' isn't a real operator). Tuck goo mines on the step so they're hidden, mirrors the same play up on the oil pit steps. Pair with a bulletproof camera/mirror on the wall and peek down each side while the mines work.
Always place the mirror at CROUCH height on this wall, not standing height — a standing-height mirror cannot see up into the hatch, but a crouch-height one can.

gadgetPlacements.location: Anchor pocket near Blue/Dirt/Kitchen hatch overlap
gadgetPlacements.note: A very strong anchor point — holds Dirt, has arguably the best angle onto the Kitchen hatch on the whole site (you see attackers before they see you nearly every time), and also holds into Blue.

Play upstairs in Logi to create a vertical line of sight down into the Freezer door — catches attackers dropping the kitchen hatch from above. A second angle from the same area covers the hatch itself if attackers approach via the window instead of the door. Be aware attackers can open the Logi hatch at any time.
A specific upstairs angle (demonstrated with an SMG-11) lets you hold Kitchen from above and also see down Main Stairs and Kitchen Corridor.

gadgetPlacements.location: Main Stairs gas canister map ingredient
gadgetPlacements.note: The environmental gas pipe near the top of Main Stairs can be shot to fully block the stairwell for several seconds (burns briefly at the end) — use it to cover a retreat when overwhelmed. A second one exists near the bottom, into Moto.

operatorRecommendations.note: Uses electroclaw to punish early hard breach attempts on the Dirt Tunnel walls, then relocates to cover the Kitchen and Moto hatches.
Mandatory info presence in Dirt Tunnel whether or not it's barricaded — source audio ambiguous between these two, both plausible given the gadget description ('a banshee, a fen rear').

Plays goo mines on the platform step and oil pit steps, pairs with a crouch-height mirror on the Blue wall. Source audio said 'Lesion' but the gadget described (goo mines) belongs to Lesion, a real defender — corrected.

generalTips.note: Contentious call #1: should Dirt Tunnel be barricaded? Creator says no (changed his mind from previously saying yes) — barricading it means the team stops playing info there, and attackers can walk all the way to the barricade for free, drone under the door, and frag defenders who have no idea what's happening on the other side.

These setups are pitched as generic rank setups that work from copper to champ, not pro-level lineups — at the top level, some of the choices here (e.g. reinforcing the Armory/Church rotate wall) would change.

Feet holes: natural instinct is to shoot what you see, but if you see feet through a feet hole, aim at head/chest height, not the feet themselves — especially relevant post-update where limb damage is reduced.
Shoot the jukebox and radio at round start — referenced as a running habit, not tied to a specific mechanical benefit beyond removing distracting audio/visual clutter.
AKs Cabinet callout — a storage cabinet used purely as a map callout reference, no longer standable-on for verticality since a past patch.
One of the creator's favourite sites to defend on the whole map.

sites.workingName: Gym / Bedroom (CC / Gym / Construction / Logi / Master / Bathroom)

reinforcements.note: Mirrors a standard CC-defense-style setup, adapted for this site.
Part of the same CC-style setup.
Reinforced as part of the CC-style setup, though the creator later suggests you could skip this one and reinforce the Gym window wall instead if reinforcements are tight.

Reinforce this wall and try to get at least some denial (Mute jammer if a battery is spare) — once this wall opens, the whole other half of the map (playing in Construction) is cut off, so keeping it closed as long as possible is valuable.
Reinforced.
Fully reinforce both.

Creator reconsiders mid-video: could skip the top red reinforcement and instead reinforce this wall, since attackers can otherwise tank bullets through the soft wall to shut down all of Master and deny the strong Master/gym-window/Condor anchor point.

headHoles.location: CC anchor wall (covers Gym window, Master window, and a third window)
headHoles.note: A line of three windows can be watched from one spot via these head holes — described as a huge anchor point, but needs something covering Red Stairs so no one can creep up on it.

rotates.location: CC rotate, opposite side from a standard CC defense
rotates.note: Placed on the closer side (unlike a normal CC-style defense) purely to shave time rotating back to peek the breach or Garage — roughly 2 seconds faster than going the long way, and lets you peek multiple angles faster.

rotates.location: Master rotate, always on this specific side of the wall
rotates.note: Placed so the rotate is hidden by a mattress from the Master window — if placed on the other side, an attacker on Master window could sit and kill anyone using the rotate the instant they come through. With the mattress-covered side, you can't be seen at all coming through.

hatches.note: Not reinforced. Standing near it once it's open is not automatically safe — attackers can see you from the roof drop-down angle. Once open, retreat to a specific covering position and wait for the drop, or hold the drop from a different spot if the breach wall is still closed.

Can hold the billiard window from this hatch — but only until attackers open the window itself, at which point the hatch position is no longer safe (classic Siege trade-off / phantom pressure concept).

gadgetPlacements.note: Play Bandit off the gym wall with two spare batteries so you can trick a Thermite/Ace charge on the CC wall (typically the first wall attackers try to open) and then run to the jacuzzi breach to trick again there.
Punch this window open at least once early, even without planning to peek it — 'phantom pressure' that makes attackers think twice about standing on Highway.

gadgetPlacements.location: Red Stairs (leading up to CC anchor)
gadgetPlacements.note: Needs some kind of info gadget so attackers can't crouch-walk up undetected (specifically flags an Ash-style crouch walk as the threat to watch for). Suggested options: Melusi (Banshee), proximity mines, or even barbed wire.

If playing Cache/Master solo and can coordinate with a teammate, put a Wamai Mag-NET here plus hold to Top Red to help a teammate out — a Mag-NET can't be played if the wall it's on gets opened first, so attackers should prioritize hard-breaching this wall early.

Difficult to prox-mine effectively here — stair geometry means attackers can crouch under the mine's trigger range without setting it off. A Melusi Banshee is a better pick; barbed wire further down the stairs also works.

operatorRecommendations.role: CC/Gym early trick + roam to jacuzzi breach
operatorRecommendations.note: Runs both the CC wall and jacuzzi breach batteries early in the round.
Plays Master with Mag-NET support if the top red reinforcement is skipped in favour of the Master/Gym window wall.
Banshee recommended for both Red Stairs (CC side) and Main Stairs (gym site) to prevent crouch-walking pushes.

generalTips.note: This is the east side of the map. Below platinum rank you can probably get away with under-defending this side, but platinum and above, it must be set up properly.
CC is not where you want to spend much time — grab a drone or two then fall back into the Cache stronghold.
Logi and Construction are both difficult to hold for more than the first minute or two before the relevant hatch/wall opens; the plan is to delay, gather info, then retreat.
Castle barricade math: a barricade breaks after 9 hits; pre-hitting it 8 times means a single punch during the round finishes it off, useful for last-second denial of a plant.
Environmental gas pipes on this site do damage (up to ~50) if you shoot them at the right angle to direct the spray toward attackers — the shot angle determines the spray direction.
Preferred over CCTV — described as miles better than the CCTV/Cash site and one the creator explicitly tells viewers to play/practice before CCTV.
Multiple environmental map-ingredient gas pipes/canisters exist across this site (main stairs, fire extinguisher) that both attackers and defenders can exploit for cover or area denial.

sites.workingName: CCTV / Cash (Garage / Cash / Construction / Top Red)
callouts: Panel One / Panel Two / Panel Three

reinforcements.note: Genuinely imperative to win this site. Combine with cage tricks (the '1' and '2' markers indicate the middle of the doors below each wall) to punish early hard breach attempts on both sets of walls.
Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Cash, top middle wall (of three on Red)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

Reinforced with two, but this wall is expected to open almost inevitably unless your team has an exceptional Bandit tricker — once open, attackers can shoot through the (now soft on one side) wall to threaten the Rafters player.

reinforcements.location: Top Red, middle of the wall
reinforcements.note: Reinforced so a defender pushed from the Construction door has somewhere to take cover.
Reinforced (this is the 10th and final reinforcement of the 10 available across the whole map setup on this site).

headHoles.location: Garage bottom walls (NOT a rotate — head holes only)
headHoles.note: Deliberately head holes rather than a rotate. If the Rafters player dies, a rotate here would let any random attacker walk straight up; head holes instead force attackers to be a Sledge/Ash/Buck-type with a breaching tool (or hard breach the wall) to reach Rafters, channelling them through the Garage window instead — which can be held from the top of Red Stairs.
Small head hole to peek the Construction door from both sides.
A couple of extra holes at the top, useful for throwing C4s and smokes over the wall toward the breach/default plant area.

rotates.location: Cash rotate (up high, vault-only when standing)
rotates.note: Attackers can't pull through it from their side. Lets a defender stuck in Cash vault through unseen to flank a planter or the attacker holding Cash door, or throw smokes toward the breach/default plant. Must check the breach angle first before using it, since it's common for attackers to hold that angle.

rotates.location: Top Red rotate, far right-hand side of the wall
rotates.note: Kept clean/flush with the edge of the reinforcement so you can peek both sides of the Construction door from it.

hatches.location: Logi hatch drop (feet holes in Construction watch this)
hatches.note: Feet holes placed to see attackers' feet as they drop the hatch onto the floor, without them seeing you.
Opened deliberately so defenders can hold the Stock door area and drop down if pressured.

gadgetPlacements.note: Ideally have a Wamai here (referred to phonetically as 'aammy'/'a wii' in the transcript, matched to Wamai given the described Mag-NET play) placing magnets along the doors below — one over the door, one on the next level along, spaced apart so a single grenade can't destroy multiple magnets at once.
Use the '1' and '2' door-center markers to line up a cage/gadget trick that covers both walls below simultaneously.
Behind the green desk is a common anchor point — covers Condor, CC window, and the breach, plus a nasty prone angle on anyone coming up Red Stairs.
Opening the hatch here lets you watch the walk-in from Stock and see the Stock door/anyone exiting it; also covers the most common below-bomb C4 spot for the default plant.

operatorRecommendations.note: Source audio rendered the name phonetically as 'aammy'/'a wii' — matched to Wamai based on the described Mag-NET/magnet gadget play; treat as reasonably confident given the gadget match, but the name itself was garbled in the transcript.

generalTips.note: If you lose Rafters, this site becomes difficult to win — attackers get direct access into Cash. Losing rafters is the single biggest turning point on this site.

The Rafters player must know exactly how far left/right they can stand without being seen through the breach or the CC window — referred to via 'panel one/two/three' callouts; Panel Two is the safest spot, though attackers know this and will target it with fire bolts.
Once the CC/main breach wall opens, defenders playing Top Red must be positioned behind the reinforcement — standing exposed near the edge risks getting feet shot from the Construction door.

Pushing the CCTV breach is considered much easier for attackers than pushing the Jacuzzi breach on the Gym/Bedroom site, due to the Gym site's reinforcement + rotate combo — so in ranked play, attackers tend to favour this site more (though pro play tends to open the Gym/Jacuzzi wall more often).
Considered the creator's least favourite site on the map — frequently over-picked by players who assume it's the strongest, when Gym and Basement are rated as stronger sites.

reinforcements.note: Same setup pattern as defending the Gym/Bedroom site.
Reinforced, mirroring the Gym/Bedroom setup.
Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Construction, single wall to the external breach
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.
Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: One spare reinforcement (flex pick, one of four walls near Bar)
reinforcements.note: Not committed to a specific wall — the creator suggests using it on whichever of four candidate walls near the bar gives an anchor player cover to hold the corridor and Kitchen approach while staying tucked in.

headHoles.location: Bar wall, top only (not fully destructible lower section)
headHoles.note: Peeking over the top of the bar wall gives a useful sightline straight down to outside the bar door — a busy approach area.

headHoles.location: Long line of sight across the cabinet area up toward Construction door (from upstairs)
headHoles.note: Covers both the most common plant spot (behind/near the vending machine) and the second most common plant spot (tucked in the jukebox or on the far side of a nearby wall).
A second, tighter line of sight — deliberately not extended too far across.

rotates.location: Long wall rotate (upstairs, over the feet holes)
rotates.note: Same setup as the Gym/Bedroom site — rotate on this wall, feet holes underneath.

rotates.location: Logi rotate, specific side of the wall
rotates.note: Placed so the mattress on that side shields you from the Master/Gym window when using it — do not put it on the exposed side.

hatches.note: Deliberately opened so defenders can stop attackers coming through the stock walls; attackers will typically instead push through the Stock door.

hatches.location: Hatch used as an escape route back into the site from Main Stairs
hatches.note: If sight control on the upper lines of sight is lost, this hatch lets you pop down, use Main Stairs, and reclaim sight into the site without forcing a door entry — try to pick off attackers through the head holes on the way back in.

hatches.location: Billiard-area hatch (holds the billiard window from above)
hatches.note: Only viable while the billiard window itself stays closed — once attackers open the window, holding this hatch position becomes unsafe (classic phantom-pressure trade-off).

hatches.location: Hatches at both ends of the site
hatches.note: Both ends of the site have a hatch, giving a drop-back escape option from either side if defenders are pushed off site.

gadgetPlacements.note: Head holes only, no reinforcement — the creator explicitly calls a reinforcement here wasted value, since defenders won't hold this spot for long given the pressure from the Cash-side approach.

gadgetPlacements.location: Strip (long line of sight to the stock double door)
gadgetPlacements.note: A good spot to hold with an ACOG-style scope — Echo and Maestro (using the ACS12 shotgun) both suggested; a DMR operator (e.g. Rooney/Thunderbird-type pick) could also work.
Castle
gadgetPlacements.note2: Use the steps to peek under a Castle barricade and spot attacker toes; playing further back or behind the bar is safer than right up against the barricade, since your head lines up with where attackers shoot through the bottom of the door.

operatorRecommendations.note: Holds the long line of sight to the stock double door with an ACOG/ACS12 scope; a DMR operator is a viable alternative.
Uses a barricade at the billiard steps to safely spot attacker feet before they can push in.

generalTips.note: Considered the worst site on the map, but can be strong with a fully coordinated 5-stack, mainly because most attackers don't know how to approach it well.
Defending upstairs is absolutely imperative on this site — losing the upper lines of sight makes the site very hard to hold.

Playing directly on-site as a static anchor is discouraged — the site is very exposed from multiple angles; better to hold from range using the upstairs lines of sight and drop back in via the hatches when needed.
Feet-hole reminder repeated from the Basement site: if you see toes through a feet hole, shoot at head height, not the toes themselves.
